FABRICATE: to invent or create something false or untrue
FACILE: achieved with ease; superficial or simplistic
FACILITY: a building or equipment for a particular purpose
FACTION: a small group within a larger one
FAIL: to be unsuccessful in achieving something
FAILURE: lack of success or unsuccessful person or thing
FAIR: treating people equally; just and reasonable
FAITH: complete trust or confidence in someone
FAITHFUL: loyal and steadfast; true to one's word
FALL: to move downward due to gravity
FALLACIOUS: based on mistaken belief or unsound reasoning
FAVOR: approval or preference; to support or prefer
FEED: to give food to; provide nourishment
FEELING: an emotional state or physical sensation
FERRY: a boat carrying passengers across water
FIGHT: to engage in battle or combat
FILTER: to pass through device removing unwanted material
FINANCE: the management of money and financial resources
FINE: of high quality; thin or delicate
FINISH: to bring to an end; complete
FIRST: coming before all others in order
FIT: in good health; suitable or appropriate
FIXED: fastened securely; not able to move
FLARE: to burn with sudden bright flame
FLAW: a mark or defect that impairs something
FLEXIBLE: able to bend without breaking; adaptable
FLIGHT: the action of flying; journey by air
FLOAT: to rest on surface of liquid
FLOOD: overflow of water onto normally dry land
FLOW: to move steadily in particular direction
FLUID: a substance that flows; smooth and graceful
FLUSH: to clean with flow of water
FLUTTER: to move lightly and quickly; flap wings
FLY: to move through air using wings
FOLLY: foolishness or lack of good sense
FORECAST: to predict future weather or events
FORESEE: to predict or anticipate future event
FORGE: to form by heating and hammering
FORGIVE: to stop feeling angry about wrongdoing
FORM: visible shape or arrangement; to create
FORT: a fortified building for military defense
FORTUNE: chance or luck; large amount of money
FRAGMENT: a small part broken off something
FREE: not under control of another; without cost
FREIGHT: goods transported by truck or ship
FRESH: recently made; not stale or old
FRIEND: person you know well and like
FRIGHTEN: to make someone afraid or anxious
FUND: sum of money for particular purpose
FURTHERMORE: in addition to what has been said
FURTIVE: secretive and attempting to avoid attention
